title: Attempted an update of FF via the 140.2.0esr.msi file, install appeared to be doing a new install (not an update) so the process was stopped and on the second update attempt it installed a new version with no bookmarks, extentions or passwords?
content:
Attempted to update FF using the 140.2.0esr.msi file. During install it asked for a location of bookmarks (seemed off, like a new install) so the process was stopped. All FF profile files appeared to have been untouched based on their dates. So the following directories: \AppData\Local\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\oeg1ubpo.default-esr \AppData\Roaming\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\oeg1ubpo.default-esr where both copied to a secondary directory as a safety precaution. Install was restarted and it appears to have again installed a new version with no bookmarks, extensions or password DB carried forward from the previous install, which is not what I wanted.

Can this be corrected, by moving the copied profile back in.

Thanks, Brian

title: What can I do if I can't find bookmarks from earlier than last  week and can't find my extensions after updating firefox

Then, I saw two firefox icons on startup menu. When I hit a faded-out one, I got into Firefox and did see my old bookmarks and extensions. When I clicked a fully colored icon, I got into Firefox but couldn't see my old bookmarks and extensions.

I saw firefox listed twice on my "uninstall" menu. I uninstalled the later one. Now, there's only the fully colored firefox icon on my startup menu, but when I click it, I still don't see my old bookmarks or extensions.

Trying to log in to my account a few minutes ago to get help, I was told the password I was using didn't work, so I came up with a new one. When I logged in after that, I got a message saying my old data might not be available.

Is there any way i can get my old bookmarks and extensions back?

So I am not sure what to do about the problems I am having but here is what happened that seemed to be the cause of the problems: Back in November I had a 2015 MacBook Pro which the battery swelled up and I found another 2015 MacBook Pro for sale and took the SSD which was running OS High Sierra 10.13.6 out of the previous computer and placed it inside another one which had a bad speaker so I took it to be repaired and they did something because they fried the motherboard then they gave me a temp computer to use which had a lot of issues and they did not return my calls so I bought another 2015 Macbook Quad core which had Catalina 10.15.7 and then I began having problems trying to get some other applications to work such as ( itubestudio, and 4k Video Dowonloader ). I have the complete Apple Pro Apps bundle but I have not had a chance to learn them yet so I may have other issues going on I'm not sure what to do. Perhaps there are some YouTube or OK Video extentions that would allow downloads... I noticed that a lot has changed in the last few years. I can be reached via phone at (818) 970-9070, and at email: sitar_man@yahoo.com.

Thank you for any asistance!

title: I used task manager to close firefox because it wasn't responding, and now my profile is gone.
content: I have been using the same firefox account for six years, and when the update that added user profiles came out, I successfully transferred all of my information to a profile. Well, today my firefox wasn't responding, so I used task manager to close it and restarted my computer. When I opened firefox again, it prompted me to select or create a profile to continue browsing. Crucially, it did not list the profile I had been using as an option, so I created a throwaway profile so look up what was going on and how to fix it. I tried logging into my mozilla account; no record of my previous browsing data, bookmarks, or extensions. I tried following the steps in the "Restore bookmarks, passwords and data from an old Firefox profile" article from Mozilla Support, but my old profile no longer appears in the profiles folder on my computer. I'm worried that if I keep messing around trying to restore my old data, I'll do some irreparable damage and never be able to get it back. Is there anything I can do in this situation?

title: Lost all history after account sign-in - how to get it back?
content:
I have a machine I've been using for months and haven't got around to archiving the history and tabs for some research I was doing. I realized this could be problematic if anything happened to the machine, so I decided to sign-in to my Mozilla/Firefox account I made years ago, so it would be safely sync'd.

Everything was fine at first, it showed it was syncing and whatnot, but then after several minutes, Firefox crashed. Upon relaunching, ALL of my history is gone! Neither signed-in nor signed-out shows any history at all, except the searches I've done just now trying to undo this mess. Did I really just loose months of history due to some bug or is there a way I can manually restore or see it from the mess of cache/profile files on my machine?

Ubuntu user, so will be at ~/ or similar for most things I assume.

title: I am unable to sign in to my bank, this just started on Feb 22, but I can sign inusing a different browser
content:
On February 22, my bank, (5/3.com), decided a transaction I was making using my debit card was suspicious and locked my account. I contacted them and informed them this was a legitimate transaction and they unlocked my account, and everything seemed to be alright, my card was working all seemed well. That is until I tried to log in and check my balance and I could not get in. I then contacted the bank customer service and spent over an hour with them attempting to resolve this issue to no avail. The person on the line suggested that I try another browser so I connected to Edge, entered my log in and password, (which is stored in my Mozilla), and it went right through which proved two things, my password and login were correct and the issue is with Firefox which is very odd to me since it has always worked perfectly as long as I have been with this bank. Again, this issue began when the bank lock my account and as a side note they did the same thing with my wife's account, she called and they unlocked her account and every thing works as before and she can still use her Firefox and gets right in to the same bank with no issues but not me. Again, this started on the date I provided above and I can use any other browser and I get right in with no problem but I am a loyal Firefox user and I want to only use Firefox so I hope you can assist me in resolving this aggravating issue!

Thank you and Kind regards, George Morris
